page: Google Cloud Platform c4n-highmem-24 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4n family. It features 24 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs) paired with 186.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of 7.75 GB per vCPU, positioning it as a memory-optimized resource allocation designed for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity relative to compute cores.

This server type does not include local NVMe storage or integrated GPU hardware accelerators, meaning all storage requirements must be met through attached network storage volumes. Additionally, the instance does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses, which requires external network configuration for public internet connectivity. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource sharing or burstable degradation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density per core allows for efficient hosting of memory-bound applications without paying for excess compute cores. The instance is configured for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, data analytics pipelines, and enterprise applications that demand a large memory footprint but do not require local disk storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4n-highmem-24 is an x86_64 architecture server instance configured with 24 dedicated vCPUs and 186.0 GB of RAM, providing 7.75 GB of memory per core. Part of the c4n family, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local storage or GPU acceleration, as it features no local disk space and zero GPUs. Network storage must be attached to meet storage needs, and no complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures stable, predictable processing. This resource profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound applications, such as caching layers, in-memory databases, and data analytics pipelines, where maximizing memory capacity per vCPU is critical to optimizing resource utilization.
